description:
You are a deployment engineer who has to prepare a self-contained Python environment for a small update rollout.

Objective
1. Create a new project directory at  
   /home/user/deploy_demo
2. Inside that directory set up a Python virtual environment named venv using the standard “venv” module that ships with CPython ≥3.8.
3. Activate the virtual environment and install exactly version 2.31.0 of the “requests” library (no other third-party packages are required).
4. Produce a one-line log file named  
   /home/user/deploy_demo/deployment.log  
   that contains ONLY the frozen requirement string for requests, followed by a single trailing newline.

Required log-file format (the automated tests will compare byte-for-byte):

requests==2.31.0⏎

Where “⏎” represents the single Unix newline (ASCII 0x0A).  
There must be no extra spaces, carriage returns, or additional lines before or after that line.

Success criteria the grader will verify:
• Directory /home/user/deploy_demo exists.  
• A virtual environment directory /home/user/deploy_demo/venv exists and contains a runnable Python interpreter at /home/user/deploy_demo/venv/bin/python.  
• Within that virtual environment, importing requests from Python works and requests.__version__ returns “2.31.0”.  
• File /home/user/deploy_demo/deployment.log exists and its content is exactly the single line shown above, with the newline terminator.

Perform the task entirely from the command line; you do not need root privileges. Avoid overwriting anything outside /home/user. When finished, the system should be in the state described so that the automated checker can validate it.  
Hint: after installing, use pip’s freeze output or `python - <<EOF` snippet to generate the required log line.  
Remember: do not print anything else to deployment.log.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful execution:

1. Directory: /home/user/deploy_demo
2. Virtual environment structure present at /home/user/deploy_demo/venv/
   Key file that will be checked:
   • /home/user/deploy_demo/venv/bin/python  (must be executable)
3. Log file: /home/user/deploy_demo/deployment.log  
   Exact contents (including final newline):

requests==2.31.0\n

The grader will additionally activate the venv and run:

python - <<'PY'
import requests, sys
assert requests.__version__ == "2.31.0", "wrong requests version"
sys.exit(0)
PY

to confirm the correct package version is installed inside the virtual environment.
